![[Pasted image 20260316094819.png]]

- An abasic site (or [apurinic/apyrimidinic site](https://www.google.com/search?q=apurinic%2Fapyrimidinic+site&sca_esv=78fb863f68fa48c7&rlz=1C5CHFA_enKR1054KR1054&biw=1440&bih=753&sxsrf=ANbL-n5c-QmvkVa_jOtlvG0PvVBu1Khmrg%3A1773668886426&ei=Fgq4aafeGZ6l0PEPz_ii8Qg&ved=2ahUKEwjF2o3Xx6STAxV3ODQIHTmfFhQQgK4QegYIAQgAEAM&uact=5&oq=abasic+site&gs_lp=Egxnd3Mtd2l6LXNlcnAiC2FiYXNpYyBzaXRlMgoQABiABBhDGIoFMgUQABiABDIFEAAYgAQyBRAAGIAEMgYQABgWGB4yBhAAGBYYHjIGEAAYFhgeMgYQABgWGB4yBhAAGBYYHjIGEAAYFhgeSJ0UUIADWLYScAR4AZABAZgBxQGgAY4KqgEDNy41uAEDyAEA-&sclient=gws-wiz-serp), AP site) is a location in DNA or RNA that lacks a purine or pyrimidine base, resulting in a damaged, non-instructive sugar-phosphate backbone
